gloss: to change one substance into another
definition: To transmute is to change one substance into another — the central operation of alchemy, moving matter across the boundary of its own nature: lead into gold, body into spirit.
history: Latin *transmutare* (*trans-*, 'across,' + *mutare*, 'to change') — the dream-verb of the whole alchemical project. Its theory ran from the Arabic mercury-sulphur account of metals to Newton's secret labors. In Magic, though, *Transmute* is a keyword — discard the card to search for another of equal mana value — so the word lives almost entirely in rules text: a mechanic, not a story.

def_sources.label: Lawrence Principe, The Secrets of Alchemy

etymology.note: The core verb of the whole alchemical project — to move a substance across the boundary of its own nature.

flavor_snippets.html: Fallen Phyrexians were <mark>transmuted</mark> into elementals by Gaea long ago, but Yavimaya's other inhabitants still regard them with unease.

mechanics_snippets.html:
Counter target instant or sorcery spell.
<mark>Transmute</mark> {1}{U}{U} ({1}{U}{U}, Discard this card: Search your library for a card with the same mana value as this card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle. <mark>Transmute</mark> only as a sorcery.)
